title: Phymatodes rupestris
taxonomy: Phymatodes rupestris belongs to the family Salicaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as willows and related species. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to western North America. The genus Phymatodes is a small group of plants that are closely related to the genus Salix. The classification of Phymatodes rupestris as a member of the Salicaceae family indicates its evolutionary relationships with other willow species. Further research may provide more detailed information about its classification and relationships within the plant kingdom.
